_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national (autopilot)
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize FL state-leg batch (Senate SD-31/32/33 + House HD-31/32): gayle-harrell (SD-31 R term-to-2028; YEA SB300/HB5/HB1/HB543/SB1718/SB4-C/HB931/SB7050 but TWO CROSS-PARTY DEVIATIONS: NAY HB1521 trans-bathroom + YEA 2018 Parkland SB7026 gun-control; 80), tyler-i-sirois (HD-31 R House Majority Leader, TERM-LIMITED + running for Brevard County Commission D2 2026; sponsor HB3, YEA SB300/HB5/HB1557/HB999 gold-silver/HB543/HB759/COVID-mandate-ban; 100), brian-hodgers (HD-32 R FRESHMAN won June-2025 special, thin record scored from endorsements: FFA life/parental/religious-freedom, Sheriff Ivey+PBA police, NRA-life; 100), rosalind-osgood (SD-32 D Baptist minister; party-line liberal NAY SB300/HB1521/HB543/SB7050/SB1718 but CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION YEA HB3 child-online-protection + REVERSE deviation OPPOSED school chaplains despite ministry; ~17), jonathan-martin (SD-33 R former prosecutor + Criminal Justice Chair; YEA SB300/HB543, sponsored death-penalty-preservation SB1604 + career-offender S1332 + 2025 immigration package; 100).

set.office: State Senator (SD-31 · re-elected 2024, 4-yr term to 2028; not on 2026 ballot)

profile.confidence_note: Web-verified 2026-06: SD-31 (Martin + parts of St. Lucie/Palm Beach) senator; first elected to the Senate 2018 (SD-25), moved to SD-31 in 2022, RE-ELECTED Nov 5, 2024 to a 4-year term — next up 2028, NOT on the 2026 ballot. Long prior FL House service (2000-2008, 2010-2018). Chairs Appropriations Cmte on Higher Education. Religion not confirmed by a primary source (secondary aggregators list Presbyterian/First Presbyterian Stuart but uncited — left unset, not inferred). Scored from HARD Senate roll calls; two CROSS-PARTY DEVIATIONS flagged.
profile.background: Born 1943. Decades in the FL Legislature (House 2000-2008 and 2010-2018; Senate since 2018). Stuart-area Republican; long tenure on health and higher-education appropriations.

gayle-harrell@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-19. Mostly standard FL-GOP voting line with TWO documented cross-party (liberal-side) deviations. HARD: YEA SB300 (2023 6-week heartbeat ban) + YEA HB5 (2022 15-week ban), Florida Right to Life-endorsed 2022; YEA HB1 (2023 universal school choice); YEA HB3 (2024 social-media/porn age-verification); YEA HB931 (2024 school chaplains); YEA SB7050 (2023 elections omnibus restricting mail/drop-box); YEA SB1718 (2023 anti-sanctuary + E-Verify) and YEA SB4-C (2025 illegal-immigration criminalization); YEA HB543 (2023 permitless carry). CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION #1 (marriage/sexuality): NAY HB1521 (2023 trans-bathroom 'Facility Requirements Based on Sex Act') — voted AGAINST the GOP biological-sex bill, scored FALSE on biblical_marriage q2. CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION #2 (2A): YEA on the 2018 Parkland SB7026 (cast in the FL House — age-21 long-gun, 3-day wait, bump-stock ban, created red-flag orders), scored FALSE on self_defense q1. Christian-liberty (beyond chaplains), economics, public-justice, and 10th-Amendment cells returned no individual evidence and are null.

0.note: YEA SB300 (2023 6-week heartbeat ban) + YEA HB5 (2022 15-week ban); Florida Right to Life-endorsed 2022 — pro-life record

1.note: Voted for restrictions, not abolition — bar not met

4.note: No documented abortion-industry money; not inferred

2.note: CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: NAY HB1521 (2023 trans-bathroom 'Facility Requirements Based on Sex Act') — voted against the GOP biological-sex bathroom bill

0.note: YEA HB1 (2023) universal school choice/vouchers — pro-school-choice

3.note: YEA HB3 (2024) social-media + porn age-verification for minors — supports age-verification

0.note: YEA HB931 (2024) authorizing volunteer chaplains in public schools — affirms public profession of faith in schools

3.note: Chaplains mapped to q0; no separate prayer/symbols measure

2.note: Voted against the 2023 budget (ILA fiscal 47%) but no balanced-budget-amendment position located

3.note: YEA SB7050 (2023 elections omnibus) which tightened mail-in/drop-box/harvesting rules — opposes mass mail-in

0.note: No affirmative pro-police advocacy meeting the high bar located

2.note: YEA SB1718 (2023 anti-sanctuary + E-Verify) and YEA SB4-C (2025 illegal-immigration criminalization) — opposes sanctuary policy

3.note: YEA SB1718 (2023) which mandated E-Verify for employers of 25+ — supports E-Verify

1.note: CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: YEA 2018 Parkland SB7026 (age-21 long-gun, 3-day wait, bump-stock ban, created red-flag orders) — supported those gun-control restrictions

set.office: State Representative (HD-31 · House Majority Leader; term-limited 2026, filed to run for Brevard County Commission D2 in 2026)

profile.confidence_note: Web-verified 2026-06: HD-31 (Brevard / Merritt Island / Cocoa, Space Coast) Republican; first elected Nov 2018; currently House Majority Leader (2024-2026). CURRENCY: TERM-LIMITED — not on the 2026 FL House ballot; has filed to run for Brevard County Commission District 2 in 2026 (leaving the Legislature for local office). Religion 'Christian' per his official flhouse.gov member profile. Cumulative Freedom Index 73%; record below is solidly conservative on life/marriage/guns. NOTE: search noise returns U.S. Rep Daniel Webster — excluded.
profile.background: Former Executive Director of the State Attorney's Office, FL 18th Judicial Circuit. Elected to the FL House in 2018; rose to House Majority Leader. Chairs the House Security & Threat Assessment Committee.

tyler-i-sirois@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-19. Solidly conservative; no liberal-side deviation on life/marriage/guns (the single contrarian HARD vote is HCR693 NO, declining the Article V congressional-term-limits convention — too narrow to score). HARD: YEA SB300 (2023 6-week heartbeat ban) + YEA HB5 (2022 15-week ban); YEA HB1557 (2022 Parental Rights in Education); PRIME SPONSOR of HB3 (2024) minors-on-social-media + adult-website age-verification (prompted Pornhub to block Florida); YEA HB999 (2025) gold & silver legal tender; YEA HB1B (2021) prohibiting COVID-19 vaccine mandates for government employees; co-introducer + YEA HB543 (2023 permitless carry), YEA HB759 (2025 lowering firearm-purchase age to 18), YEA SB214 (2A financial privacy). Public-justice role (former State Attorney's Office ED, chairs Security & Threat Assessment) is biographical, not affirmative pro-police advocacy — held to the high bar and left null. Election-integrity, immigration, and christian-liberty individual votes not isolated and are null.

set.office: State Representative (HD-32 · won June-2025 special election; seeking reelection 2026)

profile.confidence_note: Web-verified 2026-06: HD-32 (Brevard — Viera/Melbourne/Palm Bay/Indialantic) Republican; FRESHMAN — won the June 10, 2025 SPECIAL election to succeed Debbie Mayfield (who moved to the Senate); seeking reelection (GOP primary Aug 18, 2026). Real-estate broker / insurance agent. CRITICAL: sworn in AFTER the Feb-2025 immigration special session and most of the 2025 regular session, so he has essentially NO voting record yet — scored under the social-media/endorsement protocol from HARD single-issue endorsements + cited campaign positions; thin areas left null. Religion 'Christian' per his flhouse.gov profile.
profile.background: Born 1973; FAU business degree. Owner of Complete Choice Insurance and Complete Choice Realty; co-founder of the Space Coast 'Trump Club.' Won the GOP special primary April 2025 and the June 10, 2025 special general over Democrat Juan Hinojosa.

brian-hodgers@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-19 (thin freshman — endorsement/campaign-protocol scoring). No cross-party deviation; uniformly Republican. HARD endorsements/positions: Florida Family Action endorsed him (3/2025), stating he 'stands for the sanctity of life' and citing parental rights and religious freedom; endorser coverage cites his opposition to transgender participation in girls' sports; endorsed by Brevard County Sheriff Wayne Ivey, the Florida Police Benevolent Association, and Florida Professional Firefighters (pro-law-enforcement); NRA lifetime member. Scored life (q0), marriage (q2), and public-justice (q0) from those cited endorsements/positions. Per the grall precedent, NRA membership alone is NOT scored to a specific self_defense cell without a vote/clear stance — left null. School-choice, parental-consent specifics, election integrity, economics, 10th-Amendment, and immigration were slogan-level only and are null.

0.note: Florida Family Action endorsement states he 'stands for the sanctity of life'; self-described pro-life — affirms a pro-life stance

profile.religion: Baptist (ordained minister; associate pastor, New Mount Olive Baptist Church, Fort Lauderdale)
profile.confidence_note: Web-verified 2026-06: SD-32 (Broward — Fort Lauderdale/Lauderhill) Democrat; assumed office March 8, 2022 via special election; term ends Nov 3, 2026 and she is RUNNING FOR REELECTION (D primary Aug 18, 2026). Former Broward County School Board member/chair. Christian minister (M.Div, New Orleans Baptist Theological Seminary; first female ordained minister at New Mount Olive Baptist; first female chaplain for the Fort Lauderdale Police Dept, 2013). Freedom Index 6%. Scored from HARD Senate roll calls; one CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION (HB3) and one REVERSE deviation (opposed school chaplains despite ministry) flagged.
profile.background: M.Div (New Orleans Baptist Theological Seminary); doctorate in public administration (Nova Southeastern). Associate pastor at New Mount Olive Baptist Church; former Fort Lauderdale PD chaplain. Broward County School Board 2012-2022 (chair) before the FL Senate.

rosalind-osgood@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-19. Solidly progressive Broward Democrat; party-line liberal on the marquee bills with ONE notable cross-party deviation. HARD: NAY SB300 (2023 6-week heartbeat ban); NAY HB1521 (2023 trans-bathroom bill); NAY HB543 (2023 permitless carry); NAY SB7050 (2023 elections omnibus); NAY SB1718 (2023 immigration crackdown). CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION (highest-value catch): YEA HB3 (2024) child social-media + porn age-verification — joined Republicans in voting for the child-online-protection bill while five Democrats opposed it; scored TRUE on family q3. REVERSE deviation (flagged, scored against her): despite being an ordained minister and former police chaplain, she OPPOSED HB931 (2024 school chaplains), saying it 'takes their attention away from education' — scored FALSE on christian_liberty q0. Economics, public-justice, and 10th-Amendment cells returned no individual evidence and are null.

profile.religion: Christian (Liberty University School of Law graduate; denomination not publicly specified)
profile.confidence_note: Web-verified 2026-06: SD-33 (part of Lee County, Fort Myers) Republican; first elected Nov 2022, RE-ELECTED Nov 5, 2024 to a 4-year term — next up 2028, NOT on the 2026 ballot. Chairs the Senate Criminal Justice Committee; Vice Chair, Appropriations on Criminal and Civil Justice. Former Assistant State Attorney, 20th Judicial Circuit (2007-2014). Freedom Index 83% (2023) / 67% (2024). Scored from a HARD pro-life roll call, a HARD carry roll call, and his criminal-justice + immigration sponsorships; Pillar-A social cells beyond life were not individually confirmed (FI implies alignment but not scored as HARD) and are null.
profile.background: Attorney; Stetson University undergrad, J.D. Liberty University School of Law. Assistant State Attorney in the 20th Judicial Circuit 2007-2014 before election to the FL Senate in 2022.

jonathan-martin@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-19. Conservative former prosecutor; no cross-party deviation found. HARD: YEA SB300 (2023 6-week heartbeat ban); YEA HB543 (2023 permitless carry). PUBLIC JUSTICE (strongest — he chairs Criminal Justice): sponsored SB1604 to preserve Florida's death penalty if a method is ruled unconstitutional; sponsored S892 (habitual/violent career criminals), S536 (criminal-gang criteria), S890 (felony battery), and S1332 'Career Offender Registration' (ENACTED, Ch. 2026-151) — a tough-on-crime, proportional-punishment record. IMMIGRATION: designated Senate point person on the 2025 enforcement package (SB2-C/SB4-C) and sponsored S304 (violations of state immigration laws), S1278 (employment eligibility / E-Verify), S1380 (unauthorized aliens). Marriage, family-education, christian-liberty, economics, elections, and 10th-Amendment individual votes were not independently confirmed (FI 83% implies alignment but not scored as HARD) and are null.
